investments.business_description: Appriss Health (now rebranded as Bamboo Health) is the leading technology provider powering state prescription drug monitoring programs (PDMPs) across more than 40 U.S. states and territories. investments.business_description: ESO is the leading data and software platform for the emergency medical services (EMS), fire, and hospital markets, founded in 2004 and headquartered in Austin, Texas. investments.end_customers: Customers are HR and talent acquisition teams at mid-market and enterprise companies — spanning technology, retail, healthcare, and financial services — that need a scalable ATS to manage high-volume hiring workflows.

investments.business_description: LogicMonitor is a SaaS-based hybrid IT infrastructure monitoring and observability platform that provides unified visibility across on-premises, cloud, and container environments. The platform uses AI-powered anomaly detection and automated discovery to monitor networks, servers, applications, and cloud infrastructure.
investments.end_customers: Customers are enterprise IT operations teams and managed service providers (MSPs) that need unified monitoring across complex hybrid IT environments. Large enterprises and IT service providers use LogicMonitor to reduce alert noise and accelerate incident resolution.

investments.business_description: SigFig (operating under parent Nvest) is a wealth management technology company that provides enterprise digital wealth management and robo-advisory software to banks and financial institutions. The company also operates a consumer robo-advisor that creates and manages diversified ETF portfolios.
investments.end_customers: Enterprise customers are banks and financial institutions that license SigFig's technology to power their own digital investment platforms for their customers. Consumer users are individuals seeking low-cost, automated investment management through the SigFig robo-advisor.

investments.business_description: Payworks is a Canadian cloud-based payroll, HR, time management, and absence management software provider serving Canadian businesses of all sizes. The company's fully-integrated suite allows employers to manage their entire workforce through a single platform, with strong compliance for Canadian payroll regulations.
investments.end_customers: Customers are Canadian businesses across all industries that need payroll processing and workforce management software with full compliance for Canadian federal and provincial payroll requirements.

investments.business_description: Vector Solutions provides online training, workforce management, and compliance software through its learning management system (LMS) and related platforms. The company delivers industry-specific training content and operational tools for public safety, education, construction, industrial, and gaming sectors.
investments.end_customers: Vector Solutions serves over 34,000 clients, primarily in public safety (fire departments, EMS, law enforcement via its TargetSolutions brand), K-12 schools and higher education institutions, and construction/industrial companies needing safety and compliance training. Additional customers include gaming operators requiring regulatory compliance and government entities managing risk and safety programs.

investments.business_description: Trintech is a global provider of cloud-based financial close and account reconciliation software, sold under the Cadency (enterprise) and Adra (mid-market) brands. The software automates transaction matching, balance sheet reconciliations, journal entry management, close task management, and financial governance workflows in the record-to-report process. Founded in Dublin in 1987, Trintech is headquartered in Plano, Texas and serves over 3,500 clients including the majority of the Fortune 100.
investments.end_customers: The primary customers are corporate accounting and finance teams at large and mid-sized enterprises that need to accelerate their monthly, quarterly, and annual financial close cycles while maintaining audit-ready documentation and SOX compliance. Controllers, CFOs, and shared service center leaders at financial services, manufacturing, retail, and healthcare companies are the key buyers.

investments.business_description: Hanna Andersson is a premium children's apparel brand founded on Scandinavian design principles, best known for its organic cotton pajamas, matching family sets, and bright, durable children's clothing from newborn through tweens. The company operates primarily through its e-commerce channel and catalog.
investments.end_customers: End customers are parents — particularly mothers of young children aged 0-12 — who prioritize quality, organic materials, and durability in their children's clothing. Hanna Andersson buyers tend to be higher-income families willing to pay premium prices.

investments.business_description: James Perse is a Los Angeles-based luxury lifestyle brand known for its California casual aesthetic featuring premium-quality basics, ready-to-wear, and home goods. The brand operates over 50 retail stores globally and sells through its e-commerce platform and luxury wholesale partners.
investments.end_customers: End customers are affluent consumers who appreciate understated, high-quality luxury basics with a California aesthetic. James Perse appeals to consumers who prioritize materials and craftsmanship over flashy logos, typically in the 30-55 age demographic.

investments.business_description: OpenMarket, now a subsidiary of Infobip, is an enterprise mobile messaging platform providing SMS, MMS, and rich messaging services for businesses communicating with their customers at scale. The platform powers transactional notifications, marketing messages, two-factor authentication, and customer engagement programs for major brands.
investments.end_customers: Customers are large enterprises — including retailers, financial services companies, technology platforms, and healthcare organizations — that rely on SMS and messaging infrastructure to communicate with their customers for alerts, authentication, and marketing.

investments.business_description: Kellermeyer Bergensons Services (KBS) is one of the largest commercial facility services companies in North America, providing janitorial cleaning, floor care, engineering services, and outdoor grounds maintenance to large commercial clients. The company is known for serving major retailers, logistics facilities, and commercial real estate.
investments.end_customers: Customers are large-scale commercial facilities including big-box retailers, distribution centers, manufacturing plants, corporate campuses, and commercial real estate owners who outsource facility maintenance and cleaning services.

investments.end_customers: Customers are large-scale commercial facilities including big-box retailers, distribution centers, manufacturing plants, corporate campuses, and commercial real estate owners who outsource facility maintenance and cleaning services.

investments.business_description: SellerX is a Berlin-based Amazon FBA brand aggregator that acquires established third-party consumer brands selling on Amazon, primarily in Europe. The company applies centralized operational expertise and technology to optimize brand performance after acquisition.
investments.end_customers: End customers are Amazon marketplace shoppers primarily in Germany and across Europe purchasing household, kitchen, health, and lifestyle products from SellerX's portfolio of consumer brands.

investments.business_description: Thrasio (operating as Thras.io) was the original large-scale Amazon FBA brand aggregator, acquiring hundreds of third-party Amazon sellers across consumer goods categories. The company raised billions in capital to build a portfolio of consumer products businesses operated through centralized marketing, supply chain, and technology capabilities.
investments.end_customers: End customers are Amazon shoppers purchasing everyday consumer products such as home goods, kitchen tools, health and wellness items, and personal care products. Thrasio's brands compete primarily on Amazon's marketplace in the US and internationally.

The firm specializes in financing for retail, consumer, and industrial companies, including distressed and turnaround situations.
investments.end_customers: Borrowers are mid-market companies — particularly retailers, consumer goods manufacturers, and industrials — that need asset-based financing, often in challenging credit situations or restructuring contexts.

investments.business_description: WorldRemit is a global digital money transfer company that enables consumers to send money internationally to over 130 countries via a mobile app and website. Recipients can receive funds as bank transfers, mobile money credits, cash pickups, or airtime top-ups, with most transfers completed within minutes.
investments.end_customers: End customers are diaspora communities — immigrants living abroad who send regular remittances to family members in their home countries across Africa, Asia, Latin America, and Eastern Europe. Users download the app, fund transfers via bank or card, and family members receive funds locally.

investments.business_description: GlassPoint developed solar enhanced oil recovery (EOR) technology that uses enclosed greenhouse-protected solar thermal systems to generate steam injected into heavy oil reservoirs, displacing natural gas used in oil production. The company deployed the world's first commercial solar EOR project and subsequently developed the Miraah project in Oman before entering liquidation.
investments.end_customers: Customers were major oil and gas companies operating heavy oil fields — particularly in the Middle East and California — that needed to reduce natural gas consumption and lower the carbon footprint of their thermal oil recovery operations.

investments.business_description: Domo is a cloud-based business intelligence and data visualization platform that connects enterprise data from multiple sources into interactive dashboards and applications, enabling business users to access real-time operational insights without relying on IT teams. The platform supports data integration, visualization, embedded analytics, and AI-powered reporting.
investments.end_customers: Customers are enterprise companies — from mid-market to large enterprises — across retail, financial services, media, and technology that want to democratize data access and provide business-unit leaders with real-time operational and financial dashboards.

investments.business_description: Fishbowl is a professional social networking app where verified professionals can have candid, often anonymous discussions about workplace topics, compensation, company culture, and career advice within industry-specific groups. Users' identities are verified via work email or LinkedIn, creating a trusted community for authentic professional conversations.
investments.end_customers: End users are working professionals — particularly in consulting, finance, technology, law, and media — seeking authentic peer-to-peer advice and industry insights. Revenue is generated through recruiting services, employer brand advertising, and premium membership features sold to companies.

investments.business_description: Foursquare is a location intelligence and geospatial analytics company that leverages data from over 500 million devices and 100 million points of interest to provide location data, foot traffic measurement, and geospatial analytics to businesses and developers. The company pivoted from its consumer check-in app to become a pure B2B location data and analytics provider.
investments.end_customers: Customers are enterprises, marketers, and developers that need accurate location data and foot traffic analytics. Key verticals include retail analytics, advertising attribution, real estate analysis, financial services, and app development.

investments.business_description: Pluralsight is an online technology workforce development platform offering skill assessment, expert-authored video courses, and engineering analytics tools covering cloud, AI/ML, security, data science, and software development. The company serves enterprise customers seeking to upskill technology teams and was taken private by Vista Equity Partners for $3.5 billion in 2021. Following covenant pressure and a 2024 restructuring, a consortium of lenders led by Blue Owl Capital, Ares Management, BlackRock, and Goldman Sachs took full ownership, erasing Vista's equity.
investments.end_customers: Enterprise technology organizations, software development teams, and IT departments at large companies use Pluralsight to assess skill gaps, deliver targeted learning paths, and track technology workforce development. CTOs, CIOs, and learning and development leaders are the primary buyers, often deploying Pluralsight as an enterprise license across engineering and IT teams.

investments.business_description: ResearchGate is an academic social networking platform with over 25 million researchers globally that enables scientists to share publications, connect with collaborators, and access scientific literature. The platform hosts papers, preprints, and research data while providing analytics to researchers on the reach and citation impact of their work.
investments.end_customers: End users are academic researchers, scientists, and graduate students worldwide who use the platform to share their work, discover literature, and network with peers. Revenue comes from life sciences and pharmaceutical companies that advertise to the researcher audience.

investments.business_description: SnapLogic is an enterprise integration platform (iPaaS) that enables businesses to connect cloud applications, on-premises systems, data warehouses, and APIs through a visual, low-code interface with over 1,000 pre-built connectors called Snaps. The platform supports application integration, data automation, and AI agent creation for enterprise automation.
investments.end_customers: Customers are enterprise IT and integration teams at large companies — including Adobe, Workday, and Schneider Electric — that need to integrate dozens to hundreds of cloud and on-premises applications without requiring extensive custom coding.

investments.business_description: Khoros is a customer engagement software company providing social media management, community management, contact center, and customer care solutions for enterprise brands. The company serves global enterprises with tools for social marketing, customer service, and online community building. (Acquired by IgniteTech in May 2025)
investments.end_customers: Enterprise brands and agencies requiring integrated social media management, customer care automation, community management, and omnichannel customer engagement solutions.

investments.business_description: Quora is a question-and-answer knowledge-sharing platform where people ask questions on nearly any topic and receive crowd-sourced answers from experts, enthusiasts, and community members. With over 300 million monthly visitors, it has evolved into a large knowledge repository and community platform, and has expanded with Poe, an AI chatbot platform.
investments.end_customers: End users are people seeking information, advice, or expertise across virtually all topics — from technical and professional questions to personal life advice. Revenue is generated through targeted advertising sold to marketers wanting to reach Quora's intent-driven, question-asking audience.

investments.business_description: Streamland Media Holdings is the parent company of Streamland Media, a Los Angeles-based global post-production services group owned by Trive Capital and Five Crowns Capital. Operating under brands including Picture Shop, The Farm, Finalé, Ghost VFX, Formosa Group, Ingenuity Studios, and Sim Post Production, the company provides critical post-production services — visual effects (VFX), color grading, editorial finishing, sound design, and interactive content — to feature film and episodic television studios worldwide. The business was assembled through a series of acquisitions including Technicolor Post and Sim Video International's post-production operations.
investments.end_customers: Major Hollywood studios, streaming platforms, and independent production companies that require post-production services for feature films, episodic television series, and interactive (video game) content. Clients include the major streaming services and legacy studio distributors who depend on Streamland's facilities and artist talent for the final stages of content production.

investments.business_description: Alpine Acquisition Corp II is the acquisition holding vehicle for Alpine Air Express, a Provo, Utah-based Part 135 cargo airline and a portfolio company of AE Industrial Partners. Alpine Air operates a fleet of over 50 aircraft, primarily converted Beechcraft 1900D turboprops, transporting freight on contract routes across the United States. The company has grown through acquisitions including Great Lakes Airlines assets (2020) and Suburban Air Freight (2022).
investments.end_customers: End customers are major parcel and logistics carriers—including UPS, FedEx, and the U.S. Postal Service—that contract Alpine to operate regional air cargo feeder routes where larger jets are not economical. Alpine fills critical capacity gaps in last-mile air freight networks serving smaller cities and rural communities.

investments.business_description: Inotiv is a contract research organization (CRO) providing preclinical drug discovery and development services including toxicology, pharmacokinetics, DMPK, and analytical chemistry studies to pharmaceutical, biotechnology, and medical device companies. The company also supplies research animal models through its Envigo division.
investments.end_customers: Customers are pharmaceutical, biotechnology, and medical device companies that outsource preclinical research studies to CROs rather than maintaining internal lab capacity. Academic and government research organizations also use Inotiv for specialized research services.

investments.business_description: Job&Talent is a technology-enabled staffing platform that combines digital matching algorithms with traditional staffing services to place temporary workers in logistics, warehousing, manufacturing, and other light industrial roles. The company operates in multiple countries and differentiates through its app-based worker management.
investments.end_customers: Customers are logistics and e-commerce companies, food manufacturers, and other employers with high-volume temporary staffing needs. Workers are typically hourly employees placed in warehousing, distribution, and manufacturing environments.

investments.business_description: Motive (formerly KeepTruckin) is an AI-powered fleet management platform that provides telematics hardware and software to track, optimize, and manage vehicle operations. The operating company offers fleet tracking, compliance management, fuel optimization, maintenance scheduling, and AI dashcam-based driver safety monitoring.
investments.end_customers: Motive serves over 120,000 businesses across construction, field service, agriculture, trucking and logistics, delivery, manufacturing, oil and gas, and public sector organizations. Customers range from small owner-operator trucking companies to large enterprise fleet operations seeking to improve safety and operational efficiency.

investments.business_description: Blackbird Holdco, Inc. is a capital equipment sector holding company backed by Irving Place Capital (referenced in a 2019 FTC early termination notice). The underlying operating business manufactures engineered industrial or capital equipment components, consistent with the CGBD portfolio classification in the Capital Equipment industry. Specific operating brand and product details were not publicly confirmed through available search sources at the time of research.
investments.end_customers: As a capital equipment manufacturer, end customers would likely be industrial manufacturers, OEM equipment builders, or specialty industrial end-users purchasing precision-engineered components, assemblies, or machinery for manufacturing or processing operations.
